formulas.name: Principle of Equality
formulas.notes: This principle is a cornerstone of democratic societies, ensuring equal treatment under the law
formulas.formula: All individuals are equal before the law

formulas.name: Marxist Concept of Equality
formulas.notes: Marx believed that true equality can only be achieved when there are no social classes
formulas.formula: Equality can only be achieved in a classless society

formulas.name: Amartya Sen's Concept of Capability Equality
formulas.notes: Amartya Sen's concept emphasizes the importance of equal opportunities and capabilities for individuals
formulas.formula: Equality should be measured by capabilities, not just income or wealth

formulas.name: Rawls' Two Principles of Justice
formulas.notes: John Rawls' two principles of justice are widely regarded as a foundation for liberal egalitarianism
formulas.formula: 1. Each person is to have an equal right to the most extensive basic liberty compatible with a similar liberty for others. 2. Social and economic inequalities are to be arranged so that they are both: (a) to the greatest benefit of the least advantaged, and (b) attached to offices and positions open to all under conditions of fair equality of opportunity.
formulas.variables: No variables, two principles of justice
